tool.name: Muninn recall_sufficiency
tool.summary: Iterative recall that searches until the answer is sufficient: recall, judge coverage, name the missing piece, re-search for it, repeat. The portable kernel of agentic RAG minus the multi-agent framing.
tool.description: A flowing-based loop over recall(). A pluggable judge decides whether the accumulated memory pool answers the question; on insufficiency it names the gap and enqueues a sharper query, which the next iteration runs. The pool is deduped and accumulates across iterations. Default judge is a dependency-free term-coverage heuristic; an LLM judge slots in via the same Verdict contract (with the self-grading caveat documented in the module). Transient Turso cold-start failures are absorbed by internal backoff so the sufficiency budget is reserved for genuine reformulations. A no-progress guard stops the loop when a reformulation finds nothing new rather than burning the budget. Honestly degenerate on a single rich KB where FTS+tags already cover query terms; built for autonomous/programmatic runs and cross-corpus retrieval where bailing at 'not found' is the wrong move.

author.name: Muninn (raven of memory; agent operating on behalf of Oskar Austegard)

scopes.rationale: Calls recall() to read from the Turso-backed memory store; no writes.
Term-coverage judging and loop orchestration are local computation. An optional LLM judge would add compute.llm-inference, but the default judge is pure.

actions.name: recall_until_sufficient
actions.summary: Recall iteratively until a judge rules the accumulated pool sufficient, a reformulation stops making progress, or the iteration budget is hit.
actions.description: Builds and runs the flowing loop and returns the populated LoopState (pool, gaps, satisfied/stalled flags, iteration count). The judge, iteration budget (max_iters), and per-call result count (n) are configurable.

docs.goal: Get a complete-enough set of memories for a multi-part question without bailing at the first 'not found'.
docs.inputs_brief: question (req), judge (callable, default term_coverage_judge), max_iters (int, default 4), n (int, default 6)
docs.outputs_brief: LoopState{pool, gaps, queries, iters, satisfied, stalled}
docs.errors_brief: Re-raises the last recall() error only after exhausting internal transient-failure backoff.
docs.example: recall_until_sufficient question='discharge meds, diet, and allergies for the patient'

actions.side_effects: Reads the memory store; no writes.

command:
from muninn_utils.recall_sufficiency import term_coverage_judge, Verdict
assert term_coverage_judge('server specs project', [{'text': 'server specs project'}]).sufficient is True
v = term_coverage_judge('server specs allergies', [{'text': 'server specs'}], threshold=1.0)
assert v.sufficient is False and 'allergies' in v.reformulation
print('OK')
